executive_brief:
The threat landscape has fundamentally shifted from a game of attrition to a war of velocity. Over the past week, artificial intelligence has compressed the vulnerability-to-exploitation window from months to hours, rendering traditional patching SLAs obsolete. CERT-In’s emergency mandate requiring 12-hour remediation for internet-facing flaws is not bureaucratic overreach; it is a direct response to AI-assisted threat actors automating reconnaissance, exploit generation, and deployment at machine speed. Simultaneously, the open-source supply chain has become the primary attack surface, with coordinated campaigns poisoning Git tags, hijacking GitHub Actions, and trojanizing critical npm and VSCode extensions. Defenders are no longer fighting against human operators; they are competing against autonomous agents that discover flaws, chain exploits, and pivot through infrastructure faster than any SOC can triage.

The identity layer has suffered a parallel collapse. Multi-factor authentication is functionally broken against modern Phishing-as-a-Service platforms that intercept OTPs in real-time and abuse OAuth device code flows to steal valid tokens. Attackers bypass MFA entirely, maintaining persistent access without triggering secondary prompts. This convergence of AI-accelerated exploitation, supply chain compromise, and identity theft means that perimeter defenses are irrelevant. The battle has moved inside the developer workflow, the CI/CD pipeline, and the authentication handshake. Organizations that continue to rely on quarterly patch cycles, signature-based detection, and SMS-based MFA are already compromised.

threat_stories.headline: The 12-Hour Reality: AI Compresses the Exploitation Window
threat_stories.narrative:
The era of 30-day patch cycles is over. Anthropic’s Project Glasswing and the Mythos AI model demonstrated this week that autonomous systems can scan over 1,000 open-source projects and identify 10,000 high-severity vulnerabilities in a single month, with a 90.6% true-positive rate [42]. The offensive side is already leveraging this capability. CERT-In responded by mandating a 12-hour patching window for critical internet-facing flaws, recognizing that AI tools now automate vulnerability discovery, exploit drafting, and mass deployment in hours [41].

This is not theoretical. Attackers are using LLMs to chain legacy vulnerabilities, bypass modern guardrails, and weaponize n-day flaws before defenders can even triage alerts [162]. The industrialization of exploitation means fragmented security architectures are being mapped and breached at machine speed. Median patch times are climbing to 43 days while exploitation windows shrink to under 12 hours [37]. The gap between disclosure and compromise has collapsed.

Defenders must abandon static vulnerability management. Continuous Threat Exposure Management (CTEM) and automated compensating controls are no longer optional. Organizations must isolate crown jewels, deploy AI-driven adaptive defenses, and treat every internet-facing asset as compromised until proven otherwise. The clock is ticking in hours, not days.

timeline.event: Anthropic releases Project Glasswing findings, identifying 10,000+ vulnerabilities across 1,000 OSS projects
timeline.significance: Proves AI can discover and validate flaws at scale, dwarfing human triage capacity

timeline.event: CERT-In issues directive mandating 12-hour patching for internet-facing vulnerabilities
timeline.significance: Formalizes the new operational reality: AI-assisted attacks compress exploitation timelines to hours

timeline.event: Research confirms frontier LLMs suffer 70%+ attack success rates under iterative multi-turn attacks
timeline.significance: Demonstrates AI guardrails are bypassable, enabling autonomous attack chaining and exploit refinement

timeline.event: CISA adds multiple AI-accelerated exploitation vectors to KEV catalog
timeline.significance: Federal recognition that automated exploitation is now the primary breach vector

threat_stories.impact_assessment: All internet-facing infrastructure, cloud environments, and open-source dependent enterprises face immediate compromise. Unpatched assets are guaranteed targets.
threat_stories.action_required: Implement CTEM frameworks prioritizing real exploitability over CVSS scores. Deploy automated WAF/IPS rules for newly disclosed CVEs. Isolate crown jewel systems behind zero-trust microsegments. Enforce 12-hour remediation SLAs for externally exposed flaws. Integrate AI-driven threat detection capable of machine-speed response.

threat_stories.headline: Supply Chain Carnage: From Git Tags to GitHub Actions
threat_stories.narrative:
The software supply chain is under sustained, coordinated assault. This week, attackers executed a multi-vector campaign targeting developer environments, CI/CD pipelines, and package registries. The Megalodon campaign abused compromised Personal Access Tokens to inject base64-encoded bash payloads into GitHub Actions workflows across 5,500 repositories, exfiltrating cloud credentials and OIDC tokens [53]. Simultaneously, attackers poisoned over 700 historical Git tags in Laravel-Lang Composer packages, injecting a cross-platform PHP infostealer that executes automatically during dependency installation [60].

The compromise extends to core development tooling. CISA added trojanized versions of DAEMON Tools, 42 @tanstack npm packages, and the Nx Console VSCode extension to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog after attackers abused trusted-publisher workflows and cache poisoning to distribute credential stealers [176]. The GlassWorm botnet, which distributed trojanized npm and Python packages to harvest developer credentials, was disrupted by CrowdStrike and Google, but the underlying infrastructure vulnerabilities remain [131].

Developers and security teams are no longer securing applications; they are securing the build process itself. Compromised dependencies bypass traditional endpoint controls and execute with elevated CI/CD privileges. Trust in public registries and automated workflows is broken until cryptographic verification and strict least-privilege access are enforced.

timeline.event: Malicious Nx Console extension v18.95.0 published to Visual Studio Marketplace
timeline.significance: Demonstrates attacker access to major IDE extension distribution channels

timeline.event: Laravel-Lang Git tags poisoned across multiple repositories
timeline.significance: Shows attackers rewriting version history to force malware execution during standard updates

timeline.event: Megalodon campaign injects malicious workflows into 5,500 GitHub repositories
timeline.significance: Proves CI/CD pipelines are primary targets for credential and secret harvesting

timeline.event: CISA adds DAEMON Tools, TanStack, and Nx Console flaws to KEV catalog
timeline.significance: Federal acknowledgment of systemic supply chain compromise across multiple ecosystems

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Software development teams, CI/CD infrastructure, and any organization relying on compromised npm, Composer, or VSCode extensions face credential theft, secret exfiltration, and lateral movement into production environments.
threat_stories.action_required: Rotate all PATs, deploy keys, and CI/CD secrets immediately. Audit composer.lock and package.json files for tampered dependencies. Enforce branch protection rules requiring pull requests for workflow changes. Implement cryptographic signature verification for all package installations. Isolate developer workstations from production networks.

threat_stories.headline: The Death of Traditional MFA: Real-Time PhaaS and OAuth Abuse
threat_stories.narrative:
Multi-factor authentication is functionally obsolete against modern Phishing-as-a-Service platforms. Chinese-language PhaaS ecosystems now deploy AI-generated, high-fidelity phishing pages delivered via encrypted RCS and iMessage channels [187]. When victims submit credentials, attackers intercept the data in real-time and simultaneously trigger OTP requests on their own devices, capturing the code before expiration. This technique completely bypasses SMS and app-based MFA, enabling immediate account takeover and digital wallet tokenization [6].

The attack surface has expanded to enterprise identity providers. The FBI warned of the Kali365 phishing kit, which abuses Microsoft OAuth device code flows to steal valid access and refresh tokens without requiring passwords or triggering MFA prompts [21]. Attackers trick users into entering legitimate device codes on spoofed pages, granting persistent access to Outlook, Teams, and OneDrive. They then register trusted devices and create inbox rules to suppress security alerts [38].

Social engineering has evolved into automated identity theft. Signal users are targeted via SMS to steal 64-character backup recovery keys, granting attackers full decryption of historical message archives [221]. Organizations must abandon SMS OTP and basic authenticator apps. FIDO2 hardware keys and phishing-resistant authentication are the only viable controls remaining.

timeline.event: GTIG publishes analysis on Chinese-language PhaaS platforms enabling real-time OTP interception
timeline.significance: Confirms MFA bypass is now a commoditized, AI-assisted service

timeline.event: FBI issues warning on Kali365 phishing kit targeting Microsoft 365 via OAuth abuse
timeline.significance: Highlights enterprise identity as the primary attack surface for MFA bypass

timeline.event: Research details OAuth device code flow abuse enabling persistent token theft
timeline.significance: Proves MFA prompts are irrelevant when valid tokens are harvested directly

timeline.event: Signal backup recovery key phishing campaign targets journalists and activists
timeline.significance: Demonstrates social engineering bypassing end-to-end encryption via credential theft

threat_stories.impact_assessment: All organizations relying on SMS, TOTP, or push-notification MFA face guaranteed account takeover. Enterprise email, financial systems, and secure communications are compromised.
threat_stories.action_required: Enforce FIDO2/WebAuthn security keys for all critical accounts. Block or restrict Microsoft OAuth device code authentication flows via Conditional Access. Disable SMS OTP delivery. Monitor for anomalous token issuance, unauthorized device registrations, and malicious inbox rules. Deploy risk-based authentication with device fingerprinting.

threat_stories.headline: Ghost in the Machine: Mass CMS Compromises Fuel Ad-Fraud and Ransomware
threat_stories.narrative:
Web infrastructure is being systematically hijacked to fund downstream cybercrime. CVE-2026-26980, a critical SQL injection flaw in Ghost CMS, is actively exploited to compromise over 700 websites, including Harvard, Oxford, and DuckDuckGo [12]. Attackers inject malicious JavaScript payloads into the Content API, hijacking sites to distribute ClickFix ad-fraud campaigns that generate millions in fraudulent clicks [28]. The vulnerability requires zero authentication and grants full database access, including Admin API keys [54].

The attack chain extends to enterprise management systems. CVE-2026-5426 in KnowledgeDeliver LMS exploits hardcoded ASP.NET machine keys to trigger ViewState deserialization, granting unauthenticated remote code execution [7]. Attackers deploy Godzilla web shells and Cobalt Strike beacons to establish persistent control [44]. Simultaneously, CVE-2026-35616 in FortiClient EMS allows threat actors to bypass authentication and push the EKZ Infostealer disguised as legitimate Fortinet updates [184].

These vulnerabilities share a common trait: they require minimal user interaction and exploit default configurations. Attackers are automating the exploitation of widely deployed web and endpoint management platforms to harvest credentials, deploy ransomware, and monetize traffic. Unpatched instances are compromised within hours of disclosure.

timeline.event: Ghost CMS CVE-2026-26980 exploited to hijack 700+ sites for ClickFix campaigns
timeline.significance: Demonstrates mass exploitation of unauthenticated web flaws for financial fraud

timeline.event: KnowledgeDeliver zero-day exploited to deploy Godzilla web shells and Cobalt Strike
timeline.significance: Shows hardcoded configuration flaws enabling unauthenticated RCE in enterprise software

timeline.event: FortiClient EMS CVE-2026-35616 exploited to distribute EKZ Infostealer
timeline.significance: Proves endpoint management infrastructure is weaponized for credential theft

timeline.event: Rapid7 confirms active exploitation of forged VPN cookies against PAN-OS GlobalProtect
timeline.significance: Validates that misconfigured authentication bypasses grant immediate network access

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Organizations running Ghost CMS, KnowledgeDeliver, FortiClient EMS, or PAN-OS GlobalProtect face immediate compromise. Web infrastructure is hijacked for ad-fraud; endpoint managers are weaponized for infostealer deployment.
threat_stories.action_required: Patch Ghost CMS, KnowledgeDeliver, and FortiClient EMS immediately. Restrict Content API access via WAF or firewall rules. Rotate all Admin API keys and ASP.NET machine keys. Verify digital signatures on all EMS updates. Upgrade PAN-OS GlobalProtect and disable shared certificate configurations.

threat_stories.headline: State-Sponsored AI Integration and the Return of Physical Extortion
threat_stories.narrative:
State-sponsored actors are integrating AI into every phase of the kill chain while cybercriminals return to physical access for high-value extortion. GREYVIBE, a Russia-linked group, now uses ChatGPT, Gemini, and Ideogram to generate custom malware (LegionRelay), craft phishing lures, and manage C2 infrastructure [223]. Despite AI assistance, operational security failures persist, with test samples uploaded to public scanners and identifiable development artifacts left in code [239]. The group targets Ukrainian military, government, and civilian sectors using fake CAPTCHAs, spoofed login screens, and WebRTC exploits for real-time audio/video capture [188].

Simultaneously, the Silent Ransom Group has weaponized physical access. FBI warnings confirm operatives visit law firms and corporate offices disguised as IT support, convincing employees to insert malicious USB drives or install remote desktop tools [104]. Once inside, attackers pivot laterally, exfiltrate data using WinSCP or Rclone, and demand ransom for public disclosure [167]. This hybrid approach bypasses network defenses entirely, exploiting human trust and inadequate physical security protocols.

The convergence of AI-assisted espionage and physical extortion means digital perimeters are irrelevant. Defenders must assume network access is inevitable and focus on data protection, physical access controls, and rapid incident response.

timeline.event: Nimbus Manticore deploys AI-assisted MiniFast/MiniJunk V2 via fake Zoom installers
timeline.significance: Confirms state actors are using AI to accelerate malware development and delivery

timeline.event: FBI warns of Silent Ransom Group conducting in-person USB and remote access attacks
timeline.significance: Highlights resurgence of physical access attacks to bypass network security

timeline.event: GREYVIBE detailed using AI for lure generation, code obfuscation, and infrastructure setup
timeline.significance: Proves AI is now standard in state-sponsored attack chains

timeline.event: Analysis reveals GREYVIBE OpSec failures despite AI integration
timeline.significance: Demonstrates AI assists but does not replace fundamental operational security discipline

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Government, defense, legal, and critical infrastructure sectors face targeted AI-assisted espionage and physical extortion. Network defenses are circumvented via human manipulation and physical access.
threat_stories.action_required: Disable USB ports on all workstations. Enforce strict visitor verification and IT support authentication procedures. Deploy EDR tuned for AI-generated phishing and custom RATs. Implement network segmentation and strict egress filtering. Conduct physical security audits and OPSEC training for all personnel.

actor_profiles.name: GREYVIBE
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Deployed AI-assisted malware (LegionRelay, PhantomRelay, FallSpy) targeting Ukrainian military, government, and civilian sectors. Used ChatGPT, Gemini, and Ideogram for code generation, obfuscation, and infrastructure management. Exhibited OpSec failures including VirusTotal test uploads and informal artifact naming.

actor_profiles.name: Kali365
actor_profiles.motivation: Financial theft, credential harvesting, enterprise access
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Operated large-scale phishing campaigns abusing Microsoft OAuth device code flows to steal valid access and refresh tokens. Bypassed MFA entirely by tricking users into entering device codes on spoofed pages. Maintained persistent access via trusted device registration and malicious inbox rules.

actor_profiles.name: ShinyHunters
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Successfully breached Carnival Corporation (6M records) and Charter Communications (4.9M records) via social engineering and compromised employee credentials. Exfiltrated personal data, membership statuses, and internal identifiers. Threatened public leaks to force ransom payments.

actor_profiles.name: Silent Ransom Group
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Conducted in-person attacks against law firms and corporate offices. Impersonated IT support to gain physical access, inserted malicious USB drives, or installed remote desktop tools. Pivoted laterally, exfiltrated data via WinSCP/Rclone, and demanded ransom for non-disclosure.

actor_profiles.name: Megalodon/TrapDoor Operators
actor_profiles.motivation: Credential theft, CI/CD compromise, financial gain
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Abused compromised PATs to inject base64-encoded bash payloads into 5,500 GitHub repositories. Exfiltrated cloud credentials, SSH keys, and OIDC tokens via C2 at 216.126.225.129:8443. Simultaneously poisoned npm, PyPI, and CratesIO packages to distribute credential-stealing malware.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: SQL injection flaw in the Content API allowing unauthenticated attackers to read arbitrary database contents, steal Admin API keys, and inject malicious JavaScript for ad-fraud campaigns.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Ghost CMS to the latest patched version immediately. Restrict Content API access via firewall or WAF rules. Rotate all Admin API keys.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Hardcoded ASP.NET machineKey values enable ViewState deserialization attacks, granting unauthenticated remote code execution and web shell deployment.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Generate unique, cryptographically strong machine keys per deployment. Update web.config files. Apply vendor patches immediately.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Improper access control allows unauthenticated attackers to bypass authentication, execute remote code, and push malicious payloads disguised as legitimate updates.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply Fortinet hotfixes for EMS 7.4.5/7.4.6 or upgrade to 7.4.7. Verify digital signatures on all EMS updates.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Authentication bypass allows attackers to forge VPN cookies and establish unauthorized connections when HTTPS and cookie encryption share the same certificate.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade PAN-OS to patched versions. Generate dedicated certificates for cookie encryption. Disable authentication override feature.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Privilege escalation via improper handling of Redis enable/disable functions allows arbitrary script execution with root privileges.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to version 2.4.7 or later. Monitor cPanel logs for suspicious Redis API calls. Block unauthorized IPs.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Unpatched Windows zero-day vulnerability (YellowKey) affecting Defender and BitLocker. Public POC code published without vendor coordination.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply Microsoft security updates immediately upon release. Implement strict network segmentation and application whitelisting.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Unauthenticated remote attackers can pull private container images without credentials, exposing source code and infrastructure configurations.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to version 1.26.2 or later. Restrict network access to container registries. Rotate exposed credentials.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Argument injection in pull request merge flow allows authenticated users to execute arbitrary code via malicious branch names.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable user self-registration. Restrict repository creation. Disable 'Rebase before merging' until official patch is released.

hunting_leads.title: OAuth Device Code Abuse & MFA Bypass
hunting_leads.context: Attackers trick users into entering legitimate Microsoft OAuth device codes on spoofed pages, stealing valid tokens and bypassing MFA.
hunting_leads.query: | where EventID == 4624 or EventID == 4648 | where AuthenticationPackageName == "Negotiate" or AuthenticationPackageName == "Kerberos" | where LogonType == 10 | summarize count() by Account, IPAddress, DeviceName | where count > 5

hunting_leads.title: GitHub Actions Workflow Injection
hunting_leads.context: Megalodon campaign injects base64-encoded bash payloads into CI/CD workflows to exfiltrate secrets and OIDC tokens.
hunting_leads.query: | where Source == "github_actions" | where WorkflowName contains "workflow_dispatch" or WorkflowName contains "pull_request_target" | where Payload contains "base64" or Payload contains "curl" or Payload contains "wget" | summarize count() by Repository, CommitAuthor, Payload
indicators: Base64-encoded bash scripts in workflow files
OIDC token requests from unknown workflows

hunting_leads.title: FortiClient EMS Malicious Update Push
hunting_leads.context: CVE-2026-35616 exploitation allows attackers to push EKZ Infostealer disguised as legitimate Fortinet updates.
hunting_leads.query: | where Vendor == "Fortinet" and Product == "FortiClient EMS" | where EventType == "UpdateDeployment" or EventType == "PolicyPush" | where SignatureValid == false or ChecksumMismatch == true | summarize count() by Endpoint, DeployedPackage, Timestamp

hunting_leads.title: Ghost CMS SQL Injection & ClickFix Payloads
hunting_leads.context: CVE-2026-26980 exploitation enables unauthenticated SQLi to inject malicious JavaScript for ad-fraud campaigns.
hunting_leads.query: | where WebServer == "Ghost CMS" | where RequestURI contains "/ghost/api/v3/content/" | where RequestMethod == "POST" or RequestMethod == "GET" | where Payload contains "UNION" or Payload contains "SELECT" or Payload contains "' OR '1'='1" | summarize count() by SourceIP, RequestURI, Payload
indicators: SQL injection payloads in Content API requests

hunting_leads.title: Signal Backup Key Phishing Campaign
hunting_leads.context: SMS phishing campaigns impersonate Signal Support to steal 64-character backup recovery keys for historical message decryption.
hunting_leads.query: | where Channel == "SMS" | where SenderVerified == false | where MessageBody contains "recovery key" or MessageBody contains "backup" or MessageBody contains "sync issue" | where MessageBody contains "http" or MessageBody contains "short.link" | summarize count() by PhoneNumber, MessageBody, Timestamp
indicators: Links to spoofed Signal support domains

{'summary': 'Real-time MFA/OTP interception and credential harvesting via PhaaS platforms', 'severity': 'high', 'affected_products': ['SMS/OTP-based MFA systems', 'Digital Wallet Provisioning Services', 'RCS/iMessage Clients', 'Web Browsers'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': 0.0, 'workarounds': ['Enforce FIDO2/WebAuthn security keys for critical accounts', 'Delay or limit SMS/OTP delivery frequency', 'Implement step-up verification for digital wallet provisioning', 'Use email or voice calls for out-of-band verification instead of SMS/RCS'], 'references': ['Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) Analysis: 2 PhaaS 2 Furious', 'Article: 2 PhaaS 2 Furious: The Evolution of Chinese-language Phishing Services (2026-05-25)'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': "Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platforms enable attackers to bypass multifactor authentication (MFA) through real-time interception. When a victim enters credentials on a dynamically generated phishing page, the data is instantly relayed to an attacker's live administration panel. The attacker simultaneously triggers an OTP request on their own device, capturing the code seconds before expiration. Stolen credentials and OTPs are then used to provision payment cards into digital wallets on attacker-controlled devices, enabling tokenization for unauthorized transactions. Delivery leverages end-to-end encrypted channels like RCS and iMessage to bypass carrier security filters. AI-powered tools (e.g., Puppeteer) generate unique, high-fidelity phishing pages to evade signature-based detection, while anti-bot verification screens hinder automated security analysis.", 'attack_vector': 'network', 'privileges_required': 'none', 'exploit_maturity': 'high', 'availability_impact': 'low', 'remediation': 'Transition authentication infrastructure to phishing-resistant standards such as FIDO2/WebAuthn. Implement risk-based authentication and device fingerprinting during digital wallet provisioning. Deploy on-device security controls and link inspection for RCS/iMessage. Utilize AI-driven behavioral analytics to detect anomalous login patterns and rapid OTP usage.', 'exploit_available': 'yes', 'cve_id': 'N/A', 'cvss_vector': 'N/A', 'user_interaction': 'required', 'scope': 'changed', 'confidentiality_impact': 'high', 'published_date': '2026-05-25', 'discovery_date': ''}

{'summary': 'Critical unauthenticated Remote Code Execution (RCE) vulnerability in KnowledgeDeliver LMS caused by hardcoded, identical ASP.NET machine keys across deployments, enabling ViewState deserialization attacks.', 'severity': 'critical', 'affected_products': ['KnowledgeDeliver Learning Management System (LMS)'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': '', 'workarounds': ['Restrict access to the LMS to known organizational IP address ranges', 'Implement strict input validation and monitoring for anomalous ViewState requests'], 'references': ['CVE-2026-5426', 'Mandiant Report: Exploitation of KnowledgeDeliver via ViewState Deserialization Vulnerability', 'Microsoft reporting on Code injection attacks using publicly disclosed ASP.NET machine keys'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': 'KnowledgeDeliver installations deployed before Feb. 24, 2026 relied on a standardized web.config file containing hardcoded ASP.NET machineKey values used for encryption and signing. Because these keys were identical across independent customer environments, an attacker who obtained the keys could craft malicious ViewState payloads. Sending these payloads via the __VIEWSTATE parameter in HTTP requests triggers server-side deserialization, leading to unauthenticated Remote Code Execution (RCE).', 'attack_vector': 'network', 'privileges_required': 'none', 'exploit_maturity': 'functional', 'availability_impact': 'high', 'remediation': 'Immediately generate and deploy a unique, cryptographically strong machine key for each KnowledgeDeliver instance to invalidate the shared secret. Update web.config files to remove hardcoded keys and follow vendor guidance for secure configuration.', 'exploit_available': 'yes', 'cve_id': 'CVE-2026-5426', 'cvss_vector': '', 'user_interaction': 'none', 'scope': 'changed', 'confidentiality_impact': 'high', 'published_date': '2026-05-25', 'discovery_date': ''}
statistics.key_changes: Exploitation timelines compressed from 30+ days to under 12 hours due to AI-assisted attack automation. MFA bypass is now a commoditized service via real-time PhaaS and OAuth abuse. Supply chain compromises target Git tags, CI/CD workflows, and IDE extensions at scale.
emerging_trends: AI-driven vulnerability discovery and exploit chaining
Real-time OTP interception and digital wallet tokenization
OAuth device code flow abuse for persistent MFA bypass
Physical access extortion combined with network pivoting
State-sponsored integration of LLMs into malware development and lure generation
declining_threats: Credential stuffing against properly configured identity providers
Isolated ransomware deployments without data exfiltration
Traditional SMS phishing without real-time interception
